So, option (a) is the correct nswer.

content: A circular coil of single turn has a resistance of 20$$\Omega$$. Which one of the following is the correct value for the resistance between the ends of any diameter of the coil?
explanation:
The resistance of single turn of coil is 20 $$\Omega$$.

Image

The resistance of half semicircle ACB and ADB will be 10 $$\Omega$$ each.

Diagonally, they are in parallel, so the equivalent resistance,

$${1 \over {{R_{AB}}}} = {1 \over {{R_{ACB}}}} + {1 \over {{R_{ADB}}}}$$

$$ = {1 \over {10}} + {1 \over {10}} = {2 \over {10}}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ RAB = 5 $$\Omega$$

content: In a solenoid, the current flowing through the wire is I and number of turns per unit length is n. This gives a magnetic field B inside the solenoid. If number of turns per unit length is increased to 2n, what will be the value of magnetic field in the solenoid?
explanation:
The magnetic field inside the solenoid,

B = $$\mu$$0nI,

so, B $$\propto$$ n

When the number of turns per unit length is increased to 2n, then the field B' $$\propto$$ 2n

$$\therefore$$ B' = 2B

content: Which one of the following statements is correct about the magnification of an optical microscope?

options.content: Magnification increases with the increase in focal length of eyepiece.
Magnification increases with the increase in focal length of objective.
Magnification does not depend upon the focal length of eyepiece.
Magnification decreases with the increase in focal length of eyepiece.

explanation:
The magnification of compound microscope is given by the following formula, $$m = \left( {1 - {{{v_o}} \over {{f_o}}}} \right)\left( {1 + {D \over {{f_e}}}} \right)$$

where,

vo = object distance,

fo = focal length of objective,

fe = focal length of eyepiece

and D = least distance of distant vision.

$$m \propto {1 \over {{f_e}}}$$

$$\therefore$$ Magnification will decrease with the increase in the focal length of the eyepiece.

content: Colour vision in human eyes is the function of photoreceptor cells named
explanation:
Day light vision (photopic) or colour vision in human eyes is the function of photoreceptor cells named cones. Three types of cone cells present in human eye that contain photoreceptive pigments for red, green and blue lights.

Rods are the other category of photoreceptor cells for twilight vision (scotopic vision).

Blind spot is the point at which the optic nerve (transmits visual information from retina to the brain) leaves the eye. It contains no rods and cones, so an image formed at this point is not sent to the brain.

Fovea is a tiny pit located in the macula of the retina that provides the clearest vision of all.

content: The radii of curvature of the faces of a double convex lens are 10 cm and 20 cm. The refractive index of the glass is 1.5. What is the power of this lens?
explanation:
From lens maker's formula,

$${1 \over f} = (\mu - 1)\left( {{1 \over {{R_1}}} - {1 \over {{R_2}}}} \right)$$

Here, $$\mu$$ = 1.5, R1 = 10 cm, R2 = $$-$$20 cm

$${1 \over f} = (1.5 - 1)\left( {{1 \over {10}} + {1 \over {( - 20)}}} \right)$$

$$ = 0.5\left( {{1 \over {10}} + {1 \over {20}}} \right) = 0.5\left( {{3 \over {20}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow f = {{200} \over {15}}$$ cm

$$\therefore$$ Power of lens,


$$ = {{15 \times 100} \over {200}} = $$ + 7.5 D

content: The time period of a simple pendulum made by using a thin copper wire of length L is T. Suppose the temperature of the room in which this simple pendulum is placed increases by 30$$^\circ$$C. What will be the effect on the time period of the pendulum?

options.content: T will become more than 2 times

explanation:
The time period of simple pendulum is given by expression

$$T = 2\pi \sqrt {{L \over g}} $$, where L = length of wire.

Also, when the temperature is changed, the length is given as

Lt = L0 (1 + $$\alpha$$ $$\Delta$$t)

So, Lt $$\propto$$ $$\Delta$$t

where, $$\Delta$$t is increase in temperature.

$$\therefore$$ As the temperature increases, the length also increases.

As, $$T' = 2\pi \sqrt {{{{L_t}} \over g}} \Rightarrow T' \propto L_t^{{1 \over 2}}$$

$$\therefore$$ T will increase slightly.

content: Which one of the following physical quantities has the same unit as that of pressure?
explanation:
Pressure,

$$ = {{Force} \over {Area}} = {{Newton} \over {Metr{e^2}}}$$

Stress $$ = {{Force} \over {Area}}$$

$$\therefore$$ Unit of stress $$ = {N \over {{m^2}}}$$

The unit of work is joule and strain is unit less.

content: Which one of the following statements is correct with regard to the material of electrical insulators?

options.content: Electrons do not flow easily through them.
They have more number of electrons than the protons on their surface.

explanation: In insulators, the number of electrons are very less and they do not flow easily through them, very little electric current will flow through it under the influence of an electric field. This contrasts with other materials, semiconductors and conductors, which conduct electric current more easily.

content: Which one of the following physical quantities does not affect the resistance of a cylindrical resistor?

options.content: The resistivity of the material used in the resistor.
The area of cross-section of the cylinder.

explanation:
The resistance of a resistor is given by $$R = \rho {l \over A}$$, i.e. R depends on length (l), area of cross-section (A) and nature of material ($$\rho$$).

Hence, current does not affect the resistance of a cylindrical resistor.

content: Which one of the following is the correct relation between frequency f and angular frequency $$\omega$$ ?
explanation:
The relation between frequency (f) and angular frequency ($$\omega$$) is

$$\omega$$ = 2$$\pi$$f

The SI unit of angular frequency is radian/second.

content: A Kelvin thermometer and a Fahrenheit thermometer both give the same regarding for a certain sample. What would be the corresponding reading in a Celsius thermometer?
explanation:
Let the common temperature in Kelvin and Fahrenheit is X, then

$${{F - 32} \over {180}} = {{K - 273} \over {100}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{X - 32} \over {180}} = {{X - 273} \over {100}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{X - 32} \over 9} = {{X - 273} \over 5}$$

On solving, X = 574

In Celsius thermometer,

K = C + 273

$$\Rightarrow$$ C = K $$-$$ 273

= 574 $$-$$ 273 = 301

content: If the potential difference applied to an X-ray tube is doubled while keeping the separation between the filament and the target as same, what will happen to the cut-off wavelength?

options.content: Will be four times of the original wavelength

explanation:
The cut-off wavelength of X-ray,

$${\lambda _{\min }} = {{12375} \over V}\mathop A\limits^o $$ [$$\because$$ $${\lambda _{\min }} \propto {1 \over V}$$]

According to question, if the potential difference is doubled $$\lambda$$min will be halved.

$$\lambda {'_{\min }} \propto {1 \over {2V}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\lambda {'_{\min }} = {1 \over 2}{\lambda _{\min }}$$

content: Which one of the following statements is true for the relation, $$F = {{G{m_1}{m_2}} \over {{r^2}}}$$ ? (All symbols have their usual meanings.)

options.content: The quantity G depends on the local value of g, acceleration due to gravity.
The quantity G is greatest at the surface of the earth.
The quantity G is smallest at the surface of the earth.
The quantity G is a universal constant.

explanation: In relation, $$F = {{G{m_1}{m_2}} \over {{r^2}}}$$ the force between two bodies, G is universal constant. It does not depend upon the constitution of bodies or any other factor. Its value is 6.67 $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$11</sup> N-m<sup>2</sup>/kg<sup>2</sup>.

content: Why is it difficult to measure the coefficient of expansion of a liquid than solid?

options.content: Liquids tend to evaporate at all temperatures
Liquids expand too much when heated
Their containers also expand when heated

explanation: Liquids can be heated only by putting them in a container. So, when heat is provided to the liquid, the container also expands.

content: If the absolute refractive indices of glass and water are $${3 \over 2}$$ and $${4 \over 3}$$ respectively, what will be the ratio of velocity of light in glass and water?
explanation:
Given, $$\mu$$g = 3/2 and $$\mu$$w = 4/3

$$\because$$ $${\mu _g} = {c \over {{v_g}}}$$, $${\mu _w} = {c \over {{v_w}}}$$

where, vg = velocity of light in glass and vw = velocity of light in water.

$$ \Rightarrow {{{\mu _g}} \over {{\mu _w}}} = {{{v_w}} \over {{v_g}}} \Rightarrow {{3/2} \over {4/3}} = {{{v_w}} \over {{v_g}}}$$

$${9 \over 8} = {{{v_w}} \over {{v_g}}}$$ or $${{{v_g}} \over {{v_w}}} = {8 \over 9}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ vg : vw = 8 : 9

content: A positive charge +q is placed at the centre of a hollow metallic sphere of inner radius a and outer radius b. The electric field at a distance r from the centre is denoted by E. In this regard, which one of the following statements is correct?
explanation: The electric field inside a hollow metallic sphere is zero at every point because from Gauss' law, all the charges must reside on the outer surface of the conductor (sphere), hence no charge is present inside the sphere, i.e. E = 0, for r < a.

direction: The following items consist of two statements, Statement I and Statement II. Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the codes given below.
content:
Statement I The pitch of sound wave depends upon its frequency.

Statement II The loudness of the sound wave depends upon its amplitude.

options.content: Both the statements are true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
Both the statements are true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

explanation:
Pitch of sound wave depends upon its frequency because pitch of a sound is actually its property due to which we can differentiate between a shrill and grave sound.

The amplitude of a sound wave determines its loudness or volume. So, option (b) is the correct answer.

direction: The following items consist of two statements, Statement I and Statement II. Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the codes given below.
content:
Statement I Sound waves cannot propagate in vacuum.

Statement II Sound waves are elastic waves and require a medium to propagate.

options.content: Both the statements are true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
Both the statements are true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

explanation: Sound waves are elastic waves because their propagation depends on the material media, which possess elasticity and inertia. So, it cannot propagate in vacuum. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

content: The frequency of ultrasonic waves is
explanation: Ultrasonic waves range above 20 kHz which is above the audible range of human ears.

content: The magnetic field strength of a current carrying wire at a particular distance from the axis of the wire

options.content: depends upon the current in the wire
depends upon the radius of the wire
depends upon the temperature of the surroundings

explanation:
The magnetic field strength of a current carrying wire at a particular distance from the axis of the wire is given by the Biot-Savart's law.

Magnetic field, $$dB \propto {{idl\sin \theta } \over {{r^2}}}$$

where, i = current in wire

and r = distance of the point from the wire.

dB $$\propto$$ i

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: A stainless steel chamber contains Ar gas at a temperature T and pressure p. The total number of Ar atoms in the chamber is n. Now, Ar gas in the chamber is replaced by CO<sub>2</sub> gas and the total number of CO<sub>2</sub> molecules in the chamber is n/2 at the same temperature T. The pressure in the chamber now is p'. Which one of the following relation holds true? (Both the gases behave as an ideal gas)
explanation:
For Ar gas $$pV = nRT$$

$$p = {{nRT} \over V}$$ .... (i)

For CO2 gas, $$n' = {n \over 2},p = p'$$

$$p'V = n'RT \Rightarrow p' = {n \over 2}{{RT} \over V}$$

From Eq. (i), we get

$$p' = {p \over 2}$$

content: Which one of the following is the correct relation between $$\mathop A\limits^o $$ and nm?
explanation: 1$$\mathop A\limits^o $$ = 0.1 nm $$\Rightarrow$$ 10$$\mathop A\limits^o $$ = 1 nm

content: The full form of LED is
explanation: The full form of LED is Light Emitting Diode. It is a heavily doped p-n junction diode which converts electrical energy into light energy.

content: If a free electron moves through a potential difference of 1 kV, then the energy gained by the electron is given by
explanation:
Given, potential difference,

V = 1 kV = 1 $$\times$$ 103 V

We know that, energy gained by electron (E) = e . V

= 1.6 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$19 $$\times$$ 103 = 1.6 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$16 J

content:
Consider the following circuit.

Image

Which one of the following is the value of the resistance between points A and B in the circuit given above ?
explanation:
According to question,

Image

In the above circuit, R1 and R2 are connected in the parallel combination.

Hence, equivalent resistance,

$${1 \over {R'}} = {1 \over R} + {1 \over R} \Rightarrow R' = {R \over 2}$$

Now, circuit becomes

Image

In the circuit, R' and R are connected in series combination. Hence,

$$R'' = R/2 + R = {{R + 2R} \over 2} \Rightarrow R'' = {{3R} \over 2}$$

Now, R'' and R4 are connected in parallel combination. Hence, equivalent resistance between A and B is

$${1 \over {{R_{AB}}}} = {1 \over {{{3R} \over 2}}} + {1 \over R}$$

$${1 \over {{R_{AB}}}} = {2 \over {3R}} + {1 \over R} \Rightarrow {1 \over {{R_{AB}}}} = {{2 + 3} \over {3R}}$$

$${1 \over {{R_{AB}}}} = {5 \over {3R}} \Rightarrow {R_{AB}} = {3 \over 5}R$$

content: The absolute zero temperature is 0$$^\circ$$K. In $$^\circ$$C unit, which one of the following is the absolute zero temperature ?
explanation:
The absolute zero temperature is 0$$^\circ$$K. So, the relation between Celsius and Kelvin scale is

$${C \over {100}} = {{K - 273.15} \over {100}}$$ ($$\because$$ K = 0)

So, C = $$-$$273.15$$^\circ$$C

content:
Consider the following statements about visible light, UV light and X-ray.

1. The wavelength of visible light is more than that of X-ray.

2. The energy of X-ray photons is higher than that of UV light photons.

3. The energy of UV light photons is less than that of visible light photons.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
explanation:
(1) The frequency of X-ray is greater than visible light. So, the wavelength of visible light is more than that of X-rays.

(2) We know that, E = h$$\nu $$

Energy (E) $$\propto$$ Frequency ($$\nu $$), X-rays have higher frequency, so it has higher energy than that of UV light photons.

(3) UV light have higher frequency, so it has higher energy than that of visible light.

Hence, statements 1 and 2 both are correct but statement 3 is incorrect.

content: The time period of oscillation of a simple pendulum having length L and mass of the bob m is given as T. If the length of the pendulum is increased to 4L and the mass of the bob is increased to 2 m, then which one of the following is the new time period of oscillation?
explanation:
Time period of simple pendulum,

$$T = 2\pi \sqrt {{L \over g}} $$ .... (i)

According to the question, the length of the pendulum increased to 4L, i.e.

L' = 4L

New, time period of simple pendulum,

$$T' = 2\pi \sqrt {{{4L} \over g}} $$

$$ = 2 \times \left( {2\pi \sqrt {{L \over g}} } \right)$$

From Eq. (i), we get

T' = 2T

content: The connecting cable of electrical appliances like electric iron, water heater or room heater contains three insulated copper wires of three different colours - Red, Green and Black. Which one of the following is the correct code?

options.content: Red-live wire, Green-neutral wire and Black-ground wire
Red-neutral wire, Green-ground wire and Black-live wire
Red-live wire, Green-ground wire, Black-neutral wire
Red-ground wire, Green - live wire and Black - neutral wire

explanation: Red - live wire (brings the current), Green - ground wire and Black - neutral wire

content:
The graph between current (I) and voltage (V) for three linear resistor 1, 2 and 3 is given below :

Image

If R1, R2 and R3 are the resistances of these resistors, then which one of the following is correct?
explanation:
We know that,

Slope of graph $$\propto$$ $${1 \over {{\mathop{\rm Resistance}\nolimits} }}$$

In the graph,

slope of 1 > slope of 3 > slope of 2

Hence, R1 < R3 < R2

content:
Consider the following statements about a microscope and a telescope.

1. Both the eyepiece and the objective of a microscope are convex lenses.

2. The focal length of the objective of a telescope is larger than the focal length of its eyepiece.

3. The magnification of a telescope increases with the increase in focal length of its objective.

4. The magnification of a microscope increases with the increase in focal length of its objective.

Which of the statement(s) given above are correct?
explanation:
(1) Microscope consists of two convex lenses co-axially separated by some distance. The lens nearer to the object is called the objective. The lens through which the final image is viewed is called the eyepiece.

(2) The focal length of the objective of a telescope of eyepiece because objective has much larger aperture than the eyepiece.

(3) Magnification of telescope is directly proportional to the focal length of objective (fo). Hence, the focal length of telescope increases with the increase in focal length of its objective.

Hence, statements (1), (2) and (3) all are correct.

content:
A planet has a mass M1 and radius R1. The value of acceleration due to gravity on its surface is g1.

There is another planet 2, whose mass and radius both are two times that of the first planet. Which one of the following is acceleration due to gravity on the surface of planet 2 ?
explanation:
Acceleration due to gravity at planet is given by

$${g_1} = {{G{M_1}} \over {{R_1}^2}}$$ .... (i)

For the second planet,

M2 = 2M1, R2 = 2R1

Now, acceleration due to gravity,

$${g_2} = {{G(2{M_1})} \over {{{(2{R_1})}^2}}} \Rightarrow {g_2} = {1 \over 2} \times {{G{M_1}} \over {{R_1}^2}}$$

From Eq. (i), we get

$${g_2} = {{{g_1}} \over 2}$$

content: The wavelength of X-rays is of the order of
explanation:
The wavelength of X-rays is of the order of 1 $$\mathop A\limits^o $$.

X-ray is a electromagnetic wave which is discovered by German professor Rontgen. It is used in surgery to detect the fracture, organs diseased, stones in the body.

content:
Consider the following velocity and time graph

Image

Which one of the following is the value of average acceleration from 8 s to 12 s ?
explanation:
From the figure,

Image

Average acceleration from 8 s to 12 s, is given by

$${a_{av}} = {{Velocity\,at\,B - Velocity\,at\,A} \over {Time\,{\mathop{\rm interval}\nolimits} }}$$

$$ = {{4 - 8} \over {(12 - 8)}} = {{ - 4} \over 4} = - 1$$ m/s2

content: If the focal length of a convex lens is 50 cm, then which one of the following is its power?
explanation:
Given, focal length of lens,

f = 50 cm = 0.5 m

We know that,

Power of lens, $$P = {1 \over {Focal\,length\,(m)}}$$

$$ = {1 \over {0.5}} = $$ +2 D

content:
A ball is released from rest and rolls down an inclined plane as shown in the following figure, requiring 4 s to cover a distance of 100 cm along the plane

Image

Which one of the following is the correct value of angle $$\theta$$ that the plane makes with the horizontal ? (g = 1000 cm/s2)
explanation:
Given, initial velocity of ball, u = 0

distance, s = 100 cm and time, t = 4 s

We know that,

$$s = ut + {1 \over 2}a{t^2}$$

$$100 = 0 + {1 \over 2} \times a \times {(4)^2}$$

$$a = {{100 \times 2} \over {4 \times 4}} = {{25} \over 2}$$ cm/s2

Now, a = g sin$$\theta$$

$$\sin \theta = {a \over g} = {{25/2} \over {1000}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \theta = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {{1 \over {80}}} \right)$$

content: The coefficient of areal expansion of a material is 1.6 $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$5</sup> K<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. Which one of the following gives the value of coefficient of volume expansion of this material?
explanation:
We know that,

$${{area\,{\mathop{\rm expansion}\nolimits} \,coefficient\,(\beta )} \over {volumetric\,{\mathop{\rm expansion}\nolimits} \,coefficient\,(\gamma )}} = {2 \over 3}$$

$$\gamma = {3 \over 2} \times \beta $$, where $$\beta$$ = 1.6 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5 K$$-$$1

$$\gamma = {3 \over 2} \times 1.6 \times {10^{ - 5}}$$ K$$-$$1

$$\gamma$$ = 2.4 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5 K$$-$$1

content: The refractive indices of two media and denoted by n<sub>1</sub> and n<sub>2</sub> and the velocities of light in these two media are respectively, v<sub>1</sub> and v<sub>2</sub>. If n<sub>2</sub>/n<sub>1</sub> is 1.5, then which one of the following statement is correct?
explanation:
We know that,

Refractive index

= $${{Velocity\,in\,vacuum\,(c)} \over {Velocity\,in\,medium\,(v)}}$$

In first medium, $${n_1} = {c \over {{v_1}}}$$ .... (i)

Similarly, $${n_2} = {c \over {{v_2}}}$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$${{{n_2}} \over {{n_1}}} = {{{v_1}} \over {{v_2}}}$$

$$1.5 = {{{v_1}} \over {{v_2}}} \Rightarrow {v_1} = 1.5{v_2}$$

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: Which one of the following statement is correct for a plane mirror?

options.content: The size of the image of an object placed in front of the mirror is slightly less than that of the object.
The image is virtual, erect and laterally inverted.

explanation: In a plan mirror, image formed is virtual, erect and laterally inverted at the same distance behind the mirror as the object is in front of mirror. The focal length of plane mirror is infinite and power is zero.

content: An object is placed in front of a convex mirror. Which one of the following statement is correct?

options.content: It will never form an inverted image.
The image moves towards the focus when the object moves towards the mirror.
Depending on the position of the object with respect to the mirror, the image can be inverted and real.
The size of the image becomes larger than that of the object when the object is placed at a distance equal to half the focal length.

explanation:
The convex mirror will never form an inverted image.

Convex mirror always form virtual and erect image but the size of image is always smaller than size of object.

content: A circular coil of radius R having N number of turns carries a steady current I. The magnetic induction at the centre of the coil is 0.1 T. If the number of turns is doubled and the radius is halved, then which one of the following will be the correct value for the magnetic induction at the centre of the coil?
explanation:
Magnetic field at the centre of coil,

$$B = {{{\mu _0}NI} \over {2r}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 0.1 = {{{\mu _0}NI} \over {2r}}$$ .... (i)

According to the question,

$$N' = 2N,r' = {r \over 2}$$

Magnetic inductance at the centre of the coil,

$$B' = {{{\mu _0}N'I} \over {2r'}}$$

$$ = {{{\mu _0}(2N)(I)} \over {2 \times {r \over 2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow B' = 4\left( {{{{\mu _0}NI} \over {2r}}} \right)$$

From Eq. (i), we get

B' = 4 $$\times$$ 0.1

$$\Rightarrow$$ B' = 0.4 T

content: Which one of the following statement is correct?

options.content: Any energy transfer that does not involve temperature difference in some way is not heat.
Any energy transfer always requires a temperature difference.
On heating, the length and volume of the object remain exactly the same.
Whenever there is a temperature difference, heat is the only way of energy transfer.

explanation:
Practically, all the bodies around us either release or absorb energy often in the form of heat. The form of energy which is exchanged among various bodies or system on account of temperature difference is defined as heat.

On heating the matter, the energy of atoms increases and hence the average distance between them increases. This results in thermal expansion.

Hence, option (a) is the correct statement.

content: If T is the time period of an oscillating pendulum, then which one of the following statement is not correct?

options.content: The motion repeats after time T only once.
T is the least time after which motion repeats itself.
The motion repeats itself after nT, where n is a positive integer.
T remains the same only for small angular displacements.

explanation:
Time period (T) is the time taken by the particle to complete one oscillation. Time period of the simple pendulum is given by

$$T = 2\pi \sqrt {{l \over g}} $$

It is clear that the time period is independent of the mass of the pendulum. This formula is valid only when radius of point mass of pendulum is negligible compared to length of pendulum.

Here, motion of pendulum repeats itself after a fixed interval, i.e. it remains same only for small angular displacements (Q). Hence, it is incorrect to say that this motion repeats after time T only once.

content: If an object moves with constant velocity, then which one of the following statement is not correct?

options.content: Its motion is along a straight line.
Its displacement increases linearly with time.

explanation:
Whenever an object moves with constant velocity, the motion is always in form of straight line, which is parallel to the X-axis. As velocity remains same, hence acceleration of the object is zero. Also, the speed of the object changes due to change in displacement, so than in distance.

Hence, option (d) is incorrect because it is not necessary displacement increases linearly with time. It decreases linearly for constant velocity.

content: An object is moving with uniform acceleration a. Its initial velocity is u and after time t, its velocity is v. The equation of its motion is v = u + at. The velocity (along Y - axis) - time (along X-axis) graph will be a straight line
explanation:
From the graph for the equation of its motion,

v = (u + at)

Image

The object has an initial velocity u at point A and then its velocity changes at a uniform rate from A to B in time t. In other words, there is a uniform acceleration a from A to B and after time t its final velocity becomes v at B in the graph. Hence, the slope of a velocity-time graph is equal to acceleration.

Thus, we can say that the velocity-time graph for the equation, v = u + at will be straight line having slope equal to acceleration a with y intercept u.

Hence, option (c) is correct.

content: What is the net force experienced by a bar magnet placed in a uniform magnetic field?

options.content: Depends upon length of the magnet

explanation:
The correct answer should be zero. When a bar magnet or magnetic dipole is placed in a uniform magnetic field, it will not experience only force, but it is bound to experience a torque.

This torque will align the dipole moment of the bar magnet to align along the direction of magnetic field. However, if a bar magnet is placed in a non-uniform magnetic field, then it will be subjected to a force and torque.

content: Which one of the following has maximum inertia?
explanation:
Inertia means resistance or opposition offered by the body to change its state. Mass of a body is the measurement of its inertia. A body with greater mass shows greater inertia, i.e. it is more difficult to change its state of rest or uniform motion as compared to that of a body having small mass.

Hence, in the given option a cricket ball has maximum mass, so it has maximum inertia.

content: Which one of the following is the value of 1 kWh of energy converted into joules?
explanation:
1 kWh is the unit of electrical power.

1 kWh = 1000 W $$\times$$ 3600 s ($$\because$$ 1 J = 1 W $$\times$$ 1 s)

= 3.6 $$\times$$ 106 J

content: Which one of the following statement about gravitational force is not correct?

options.content: It is experienced by all bodies in the universe.
It is a dominant force between celestial bodies.
It is a negligible force for atoms.
It is same for all pairs of bodies in our universe.

explanation:
Gravitational force is defined as a non-contact force of attraction between any two bodies in universe (no matter how far the bodies are).

It is experienced by all bodies in the universe. The celestial bodies experienced these forces, but it is not same for all pairs of bodies in our universe.

So, option (d) is not a correct statement.

content: Whether an object will float or sink in a liquid, depends on

options.content: mass of the object and density of liquid only
difference in the densities of the object and liquid
mass and shape of the object only

explanation:
Whether an object sinks or floats depends on the density of the object and the fluid in which it is immersed.

An object that is denser than a fluid will sink in the fluid while an object that is less dense will float. A floating object is said to be buoyant.

The classical Greek inventor Archimedes' was first to understand that buoyancy is a force.

Archimedes' principle states that any object immersed in or floating in a fluid is buoyed up by a force equal to the weight of displaced fluid.

content: Which one of the following device is non-ohmic?
explanation:
According to Ohm's law, the electric current flowing through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference applied across its ends, provided the physical condition (such as temperature) remains unchanged.

Hence, the device which follows Ohm's law for all voltage across it is called as an ohmic device. For example, a wire rheostat conducting copper coil, electric heating coil, etc.

The device that does not follow Ohm's law is known as non-ohmic device. For example, semi-conductor diode.

content: Which one of the following is the natural phenomenon based on a simple periscope works?
explanation: Periscope is an optical instrument which is based on the phenomenon of reflection of light. Light is reflected from the first mirror into the second one at 45$$^\circ$$ angle which is then reflected to the eye of the viewer.

content: Which one of the following frequency range is sensitive to human ears?
explanation: 20-20000 Hz is the frequency range which is sensitive to human ears. The audible frequency range to which human ears can respond is 20-20000 Hz. The sound frequency beyond this limit may damage ear drums and cause hearing disabilities in humans.

content:
Which of the following statement(s) about a fluid at rest in a cup is/are correct ?

1. Pressure is same at all the points in the fluid.

2. Pressure is exerted on the walls.

3. Pressure exists everywhere in the fluid.
explanation:
When a fluid is at rest in a cup then pressure is same at all the points in the fluid, pressure is exerted on the walls and pressure exists everywhere in the fluid.

The pressure at a point within a volume of fluid means that the point to be surrounded by a small container by the area of the container.

Hence, all the statements are correct.

content: Which one of the following device is used to measure atmospheric pressure?
explanation:
Barometer is used to measure the atmospheric pressure. The function of a barometer is to know whether or not atmospheric pressure is rising or falling.

Ammeter is a device which is used to measure electric current in amperes.

Potentiometer is used to measure the emf of a cell.

Lactometer is used to measure the purity of milk.

content:
Which of the following statement(s) about latent heat for a given substance is/are correct?

1. It is fixed at a given temperature.

2. It depends upon the temperature and volume.

3. It is independent of temperature and volume.

4. It depends on the temperature but independent of volume.
explanation: The amount of heat per unit mass transferred during change of state of the substance is called latent heat of the substance. It is always fixed at a given temperature and does not change with temperature and volume of the substance, i.e. it is independent of temperature and volume. e.g. Latent heat of ice is 3.34 $$\times$$ 10<sup>5</sup> J/kg. So, option (b) is the correct answer.

content: Which one of the following statement about the mass of a body is correct?

options.content: It changes from one place to another.
It does not depend on its temperature.

explanation: Mass is a quantity of matter present inside the body which measures the inertia of a body. The mass of the body cannot be zero. Irrespective of the position of body in the universe, mass of the body always remains constant everywhere, which is independent of its temperature.

content: A ball balanced on a vertical rod is an example of
explanation:
When the centre of gravity of a body lies below point of suspension or support, the body is said to be in stable equilibrium. When the centre of gravity of a body lies above the point of suspension or support, the body is said to be in unstable equilibrium.

A balanced ball on a vertical rod is an example of unstable equilibrium because when the rod is slightly disturbed its centre of gravity is lowered.

The line of action of its weight lies outside the base of the rod. Then, the torque due to weight, toppled it down.

When the centre of gravity of a body lies at the point of suspension, the body is said to be inventral equilibrium.

content:
Which of the following statement(s) about specific heat of a body is/are correct?

1. It depends upon mass and shape of the body.

2. It is independent of mass and shape of the body.

3. It depends only upon the temperature of the body.
explanation:
Every substance has a unique value for the amount of heat absorbed or rejected to change the temperature of unit mass of it by one unit. This quantity is referred as the specific heat capacity of a body.

it depends on the nature as well as mass of the substance and it changes in temperature.

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: Which one of the following is an example of the force of gravity of the earth acting on a vibrating pendulum bob?
explanation:
An object that is vibrating is acted upon by a restoring force. The restoring force causes the vibrating object to slow down as it moves away from the equilibrium position and to speed up as it approaches the equilibrium position.

There is the force of gravity that acts downward upon the bob and the tension force results from string pulling upon the bob of the pendulum.

content: Which one of the following statement about the refractive index of a material medium with respect to air is correct?

options.content: It can be either positive or negative.
It is unity for all materials.
It is always greater than one.

explanation: Refractive index of a material is given by the ratio of speed of light in vacuum to the speed of light in any medium. Refractive index is always greater than one because by the definition of refractive index, (n) of a medium (e.g. glass) is the ratio of the speed of light (c), in a vacuum to the speed in the medium v and speed of light is maximum in vacuum, which gives the value of n greater than one.

content: Which one of the following statement about magnetic field lines is not correct?

options.content: They can emanate from a point.
They do not cross each other.
Field lines between two poles cannot be precisely straight lines at the ends.
There are no field lines within a bar magnet.

explanation: The magnetic field lines is defined as the path along which the compass needless are aligned. They cannot intersect i.e. do not cross each other. Tangent drawn at any point gives the direction of magnetic field. Outside a magnet, they are directed from north to south pole and inside a magnet they are directed from south to north. These lines are always normal to the surface, which are precisely straight at the ends. Hence, statement (c) is not correct.

content: Two convex lenses with power 2D are kept in contact with each other. The focal length of the combined lens system is
explanation:
Here, P1 = P2 = + 2D

Using the formula, total power,

P = P1 + P2 = 2 + 2 = + 4D

$$\therefore$$ Focal length, $$f = {1 \over P} = {1 \over 4} = 0.25$$ m

content:
Which of the following statement(s) about electromagnetic waves, sound waves and water waves is/are correct?

1. They exhibit reflection.

2. They carry energy.

3. They exert pressure.

4. They can travel in vacuum.
explanation:
Electromagnetic waves, sound waves and water waves exhibit reflection, carry energy (EM waves), exert pressure (water waves), but sound waves cannot travel in vacuum.

Hence, statements 1, 2 and 3 are correct but 4 is incorrect.

content: Thermal capacity of a body depends on the

options.content: mass and shape of the body only
mass, shape and temperature of the body

explanation: Thermal capacity of a body can be defined as the amount of heat required to change the temperature of an object by a certain degree. It depends upon the mass, shape and temperature of the body.

content: Which one of the following metal is used in the filaments of photoelectric cells that converts light energy into electric energy?
explanation: Tungsten is used in the filament of photoelectric cells that converts light energy into electric energy. Main type of these cells are photo voltaic cell, photo emissive cell, photo conductive cell. Electrons are obtained from the filament by thermionic emission and are accelerated to the anode by a potential difference ($$ \sim $$ 100 kV).

content: A thin disc and a thin ring, both have mass M and radius R. Both rotate about axes through their centre of mass and are perpendicular to their surfaces at the same angular velocity. Which one of the following statements is correct?

options.content: The ring has higher kinetic energy.
The disc has higher kinetic energy.
The ring and the disc both have the same kinetic energy.
Kinetic energies of both the bodies are zero, since they are not in linear motion.

explanation:
Given, a thin disc and a thin ring have equal mass and radius.

Angular velocity of thin disc - Angular velocity of thin ring

$$\omega$$ring = $$\omega$$disc

$$\therefore$$ Kinetic energy of ring = Kinetic energy of disc

$${1 \over 2}{I_{ring}}\omega _{ring}^2 = {1 \over 2}{I_{disc}}\omega _{disc}^2$$

$${1 \over 2}(M{R^2})\omega _{ring}^2 = {1 \over 2}\left( {{{M{R^2}} \over 2}} \right)\omega _{disc}^2$$

($$\because$$ $${I_{ring}} = M{R^2}$$ or $${I_{disc}} = {1 \over 2}M{R^2}$$)

$$\omega _{ring}^2 = {{\omega _{disc}^2} \over 2}$$

$$2\omega _{ring}^2 = \omega _{disc}^2$$

Hence, $$K{E_{ring}} > K{E_{disc}}$$

So, the ring has higher kinetic energy.

content: In which of the following phenomena do heat waves travel along a straight line with the speed of light?
explanation: Thermal radiations are heat waves (infrared waves) which are electromagnetic waves. Electromagnetic waves travel in straight line with the speed of light. It depends on the temperature of the body and nature of radiating surface of the body. Hence, option (c) is correct.

content: Which one of the following statements regarding Ohm's law is not correct?

options.content: Ohm's law is an assumption that current through a conductor is always directly proportional to the potential difference applied to it.
A conducting device obeys Ohm's law when the resistance of a device is independent of magnitude and polarity of applied potential difference.
A conducting material obeys Ohm's law when the resistance of material is independent of the magnitude and direction of applied electric field.
All homogeneous materials obey Ohm's law irrespective of whether the field is within range or strong.

explanation: Statement (d) is not correct, because in strong electric field, heating effect will dominant and here is deviations of curve, i.e. it does not obey Ohm's law.

content:
When a ball bounces off the ground, which of the following changes suddenly?

(Assume no loss of energy to the floor)
explanation:
When a ball bounces off the ground, there will be sudden change in the momentum of the ball.

$$ \bullet $$ It includes the mass and the velocity of the object.

$$ \bullet $$ It shows the sudden change of the velocity of the object according to its mass.

$$ \bullet $$ If the mass is large, the change will be greater and if it is small, then the velocity will be lesser.

content: If an object moves at a non-zero constant acceleration for a certain interval of time, then the distance it covers in that time

options.content: is independent of its initial velocity

explanation:
If object moving with non-zero constant acceleration, then the displacement covered by it, is given by equation

$$s = ut + {1 \over 2}a{t^2}$$

where, s = displacement, u = initial velocity, a = acceleration and t = time.

Hence, from above equation, displacement is dependent to initial velocity.

Distance covered is a non-liner curve of time.

content: A solid disc and a solid sphere have the same mass and same radius. Which one has the higher moment of inertia about its centre of mass?

options.content: Both have the same moment of inertia
The information provided is not sufficient to answer the question

explanation:
As we know, moment of the inertia

$${I_{disc}} = {1 \over 2}M{R^2}$$ and $${I_{sphere}} = {2 \over 5}M{R^2}$$

$$\because$$ Given, $${M_{disc}} = {M_{sphere}}$$ and $${R_{disc}} = {R_{sphere}}$$

So, $${{{I_{disc}}} \over {{I_{sphere}}}} = {{{1 \over 2}M{R^2}} \over {{2 \over 5}M{R^2}}} = {5 \over 4}$$ ..... (i)

Hence, from Eq. (i), we get

Idisc > Isphere

So, the disc has higher moment of inertia.

content: Two substances of densities $$\rho$$<sub>1</sub> and $$\rho$$<sub>2</sub> are mixed in equal volume and their relative density is 4. When they are mixed in equal masses, relative density is 3. The values of $$\rho$$<sub>1</sub> and $$\rho$$<sub>2</sub> respectively, are
explanation:
When substances are mixed in equal volume.

Then, relative density,

$$4 = {{V{\rho _1} + V{\rho _2}} \over {V + V}}$$ [$$\because$$ volume(V) $$\times$$ density($$\rho$$) = mass(M)]

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\rho$$1 + $$\rho$$2 = 8 ..... (i)

Similarly, when substances are mixed in equal mass.

Relative density,

$$3 = {{M + M} \over {{M \over {{\rho _1}}} + {M \over {{\rho _2}}}}} = {{2{\rho _1}{\rho _2}} \over {{\rho _1} + {\rho _2}}}$$ ..... (ii)

Hence, from Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$\rho$$1 = 6 and $$\rho$$2 = 2

content: A car starts from Bengaluru, goes 50 km in a straight line towards South, immediately turns around and returns to Bengaluru. The time taken for this round trip is 2h. The magnitude of the average velocity of the car for this round trip

options.content: cannot be calculated without knowing accelration

explanation:
According to question, a round trip is mentioned by a car. But as we know, for round trip the displacement is zero. So,

$${V_{av}} = {{displacement} \over {time}} = {0 \over 2} = 0$$ ms$$-$$1

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: "The sum of emf's and potential differences around a closed loop equals zero" is a consequence of
explanation:
It is Kirchhoff's second law also known as loop's law.

i.e. $$\Sigma$$E = iR

It is based on conservation of energy, since work done in a closed path is zero.

content: A student measures certain lengths using a meter scale having least count equal to 1 mm. Which one of the following measurement is more precise?
explanation:
Using instrument of higher precision improving experimental techniques, etc., we can reduce the least count error.

Here, option (c) 0.925 mm is more precise than other.

content: If the work done on the system or by the system is zero, then which one of the following statement for a gas kept at a certain temperature is correct?

options.content: Change in internal energy of the system is equal to flow of the hat in or out of the system.
Change in internal energy of the system is less than heat transferred.
Change in internal energy of the system is more than the heat flow.

explanation:
For first law of thermodynamics,

$$\Delta$$Q = $$\Delta$$U + $$\Delta$$W

where, $$\Delta$$Q = heat, $$\Delta$$U = change in internal energy and $$\Delta$$W = work done.

Since, $$\Delta$$W = 0

So, $$\Delta$$Q = $$\Delta$$U

Hence, there is change in internal energy of the system is equal to flow of heat in or out of the system, option (a) is correct.

content:
Consider the following part of an electric circuit.

Image

The total electrical resistance in the given part of the electric circuit is
explanation:
There are three resistances (2$$\Omega$$, 4$$\Omega$$ and 8$$\Omega$$) are in parallel.

So, $${1 \over {{R_1}}} = {1 \over 2} + {1 \over 4} + {1 \over 8} \Rightarrow {R_1} = {8 \over 7}\Omega $$

Now, R1 is in series with 1$$\Omega$$ resistance.

So, $${R_{eq}} = 1\Omega + {R_1} = 1 + {8 \over 7} = {{15} \over 7}\Omega $$

Hence, option (b) is correct.

content: Which one of the following statements regarding lenses is not correct?

options.content: A convex lens produces both real and virtual images.
A concave lens produces both real and virtual images.
A convex lens can produce images equal, greater and smaller than the size of the object.
A concave lens always produces images smaller than the size of the object.

explanation:
A concave lens always forms virtual image, whereas convex lens forms both real and virtual images.

Hence, option (b) is incorrect.

content: 10 g of ice at $$-$$10$$^\circ$$ C is mixed with 10 g of water at 0$$^\circ$$ C. The amount of heat required to raise the temperature of mixture to 10$$^\circ$$ C is
explanation:
Heat require by 10g of water at $$-$$10$$^\circ$$ C to 0$$^\circ$$ C is

H1 = ms$$\Delta$$$$\theta$$ = 10 $$\times$$ (0.50) (0 $$-$$ ($$-$$10) = 50 cal

Now, latent heat of melting of 10g of ice

H2 = mL = 10 $$\times$$ 80 = 800 cal

Now, there is 20g of water at 0$$^\circ$$ C, so heat required to reach the temperature of +10$$^\circ$$ C

H3 = ms$$\Delta$$$$\theta$$ = 20 $$\times$$ 1 $$\times$$ (10 $$-$$ 0) = 200 cal

Total heat required,

H = H1 + H2 + H3

= 50 + 800 + 200 = 1050 cal

content: Soaps clean surfaces on the principle of based on
explanation: Soaps clean surfaces on the principle of based on surface tension. Soap is a surface active agent or surfactant. It helps to reduce the surface tension of water and thereby speed up the cleaning process of soaps.

content:
Water is heated with a coil of resistance R connected to domestic supply. The rise of temperature of water will depend on

1. supply voltage

2. current passing through the coil

3. time for which voltage is supplied

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
explanation:
Heat generated by a resistance coil is given by

H = I2Rt

From Ohm's law, $$I = {V \over R}$$

So, $$H = I\left( {{V \over R}} \right)Rt = IVt$$

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: A pendulum clock is lifted to a height where the gravitational acceleration has a certain value of g. Another pendulum clock of same length, but of double the mass of the bob is lifted to another height where the gravitational acceleration is $${g \over 2}$$. The time period of the second pendulum would be (in terms of period T of the first pendulum)
explanation:
Time period of pendulum,

$$T = 2\pi \sqrt {{I \over g}} $$ ........ (i)

When acceleration due to gravity changed to $${g \over 2}$$, then time period,

$$T' = 2\pi \sqrt {{I \over {g/2}}} = \sqrt 2 \left( {2\pi \sqrt {{I \over g}} } \right)$$ [from Eq. (i)]

$$ \Rightarrow T' = \sqrt 2 T$$

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: The loudness of sound depends upon the

options.content: velocity of sound waves in the medium
frequency and velocity of the sound waves

explanation: Loudness of sound wave is depend on its amplitude, while sharpness of sound wave is depend on its frequency. Hence, option (b) is correct.

content: Two bodies of mass M each are placed R distance apart. In another system, two bodies of mass 2M each are placed $${R \over 2}$$ distance apart. If F be the gravitational force between the bodies in the first system, then the gravitational force between the bodies in the second system will be
explanation:
Situation I,

Force between the masses

Image

$$F = {{GMM} \over {{R^2}}} = {{G{M^2}} \over {{R^2}}}$$ ..... (i)

Situation II,

Force between the masses

Image

$$F' = {{G(2M)(2M)} \over {{{\left( {{R \over 2}} \right)}^2}}} = {{16G{M^2}} \over {{R^2}}}$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

F' = 16F

Hence, option (a) is correct.

content: Light rays move in straight lines. But through an optical fibre, they can move in any type of zig-zag path because

options.content: the holes through the fibres are extremely fine
light rays are absorbed at the entry end and relieved at the exit end of the fibre
scattering of light occurs inside the fibre
successive total internal reflections occur as a ray moves through the fibre

explanation:
The working of optical fibre is based on the total internal reflection. Its inner part is core of higher refractive index surrounded by another layer of glass of lower refractive index. It is surrounded by plastic jacket.

When light enters from the one end of the core and moves towards cladding, then total internal reflection takes place again and again and light propagate through it. Optical fibres are used in decorative table lamps.

content: Compared to audible sound waves, ultrasound waves have
explanation: Ultrasound waves have higher frequency than audible sound waves. The audible range of sound for human beings is form 20 to 20,000 Hz. Children under the age of 5 and some animals such as dogs can hear upto 25,000 Hz. As people grow older, their ears become less sensitive to higher frequency. Ultrasonic sound of frequency higher than 20,000 Hz, which cannot be heard by human beings.

content: A rigid body of mass 2 kg is dropped from a stationary balloon kept at a height of 50 m from the ground. The speed of the body when it just touches the ground and the total energy when it is dropped from the balloon are respectively. (Take, acceleration due to gravity, g = 9.8 m/s<sup>2</sup>)
explanation:
Given, M = 2 kg and height = 50 m

Speed of body at the ground,

v2 $$-$$ u2 = 2gH

v = $$\sqrt {2gH} $$ [$$\because$$ u = 0]

= $$\sqrt {2\times9.8\times50} $$

$$\Rightarrow$$ v = $$\sqrt {980} $$ ms$$-$$1

Hence, the total energy = kinetic energy

$$\Rightarrow$$ TE = $${1 \over 2}$$Mv2

= $${1 \over 2}$$ $$\times$$ 2 $$\times$$ ($$\sqrt {980} $$)2 = 980 J

Hence, option (d) is correct.

content: The temperature of a place on one sunny day is 113 in Fahrenheit scale. The Kelvin scale reading of this temperature will be
explanation:
Given, temperature,

Tf = 113 F

As, we know

$${T_k} = 273 + {{10} \over {18}}({T_f} - 32)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {T_k} = 273 + {{10} \over {18}}(113 - 32)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {T_k} = 318$$ K

content: Radiations coming from the sun are mostly in the form of

options.content: Both short and long wavelength infrared

explanation:
The mode of transfer of heat from one body to another without any actual movement the particles involved and without heating the intervening medium, is called radiation. For radiation, there is no need of any medium because it is of electromagnetic nature.

So, radiation coming from the sun are mostly in form of light and long wavelength infrared.

content:
Which of the following are the characteristics of electromagnetic waves ?

1. They are elastic waves.

2. They can also move in vacuum.

3. They have electric and magnetic components which are mutually perpendicular.

4. They move with a speed equal to 3 lakh meters per second.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
explanation:
Electromagnetic waves of EM waves are waves that are created as a result of vibrations between an electric field and a magnetic field. They are formed when an electric field comes in contact with a magnetic field. The electric field and magnetic field of an electromagnetic wave are perpendicular (at right angles) to each other. They are also perpendicular to the direction of the EM wave.

These waves travel with a constant velocity of 3.0 $$\times$$ 108 m/s in vacuum. They are deflected neither by the electric field nor by the magnetic field.

Hence, statements 2 and 3 are correct.

content: The light energy escaping from the sun can be spread by

options.content: a combination of a convex lens and a concave lens

explanation:
The light energy escaping from the sun can be spread by a shower of rain drops. The sunlight shines on a water droplet. As the light passes into the droplet, the light bends or refracts, a little because light travels slower in water than in air (because water is denser). Then, the light bounces off the back of water droplet and goes back the way it came, bending again as its speeds up when it exist the water droplet.

Hence, the light energy escaping from the sun can be spread. A disperse the light into VIBGYOR and spread it.

content: The correct sequence of energy transfer that occurs when an apple falls to the ground is

options.content: gravitational potential energy $$\to$$ heat energy to air $$\to$$ kinetic energy $$\to$$ heat energy to ground and apple $$\to$$ sound energy
gravitational potential energy $$\to$$ sound energy $$\to$$ kinetic energy $$\to$$heat energy to air $$\to$$ heat energy to ground and apple
gravitational potential energy $$\to$$ kinetic energy $$\to$$ heat energy to air $$\to$$ heat energy to ground and apple $$\to$$sound energy
gravitational potential energy $$\to$$kinetic energy $$\to$$ sound energy $$\to$$ heat energy to air $$\to$$ heat energy to ground and apple

explanation: When apple is on highest position, then it has only gravitational potential energy. When, it falls to the ground the first its gravitational potential energy starts converting into kinetic energy. Due to air friction some kinetic energy losses and converts into heat energy to air. When apple falls on the ground, then its remaining energy converts into heat energy to ground and sound energy. Hence, option (c) is correct.

content: Which one of the following mineral is used as a fuel in nuclear power stations?
explanation: Uraninite, formerly known as pitchblende is a radioactive uranium rich mineral which is used as a fuel in nuclear power stations. It is an ore with a chemical composition that is largely UO<sub>2</sub>, but due to oxidation the mineral typically contains variable proportions of U<sub>3</sub>O<sub>8</sub>.

content: Which one of the following can charge an insulator?
explanation: Static electricity can charge an insulator. Charging of insulator by induction process, static charge is required. A static electric charge can be created whenever two surfaces contact and separate at least one of the surfaces has a high resistance to electric current.

content: At 20$$^\circ$$C, the speed of sound in water is approximately
explanation: The speed of sound in water at 20$$^\circ$$C is approximately 1500 m/s.

content: Let us consider a copper wire having radius r and length l. Let its resistance be R. If the radius of another copper wire is 2r and the length is l/2, then the resistance of this wire will be
explanation:
Given, length of copper wire = l and radius of copper wire = r

$$\therefore$$ Resistance,

$$R = \rho {l \over A} = \rho .{l \over {\pi {r^2}}}$$ ($$\because$$ A = $$\pi$$r2) ..... (i)

For second wire,

length, $${l_1} = {l \over 2}$$ and radius, r1 = 2r

Resistance of wire,

$${R_1} = \rho .{{{l_1}} \over {\pi {r_1}^2}} = \rho .{{l/2} \over {\pi {{(2r)}^2}}}$$

$$ = \rho .{l \over {2 \times \pi .4{r^2}}} = {1 \over 8}.\rho .{l \over {\pi {r^2}}}$$

$$ = {1 \over 8}.R$$ [from Eq. (i)]

$$ = {R \over 8}$$

content: Basic scientific principle behind a nuclear reactor is
explanation: Basic scientific principle behind a nuclear reactor is controlled nuclear fission, in which a heavy atomic nucleus splits into two smaller nuclei and a large amount of energy is released in this process.

content: Who among the following has explained the phenomenon of photoelectric effect?
explanation: The experiment of photoelectric effect is performed by Heinrich Hertz, but the phenomenon was explained by Albert Einstein. It is the phenomenon of ejection of electrons from the structure of a metal when light of suitable frequency strikes it.

content: Two metallic wires A and B are made using copper. The radius of wire A is r while its length is l. A DC voltage V is applied across the wire A, causing power dissipation P. The radius of wire B is 2r and its length is 2l and the same DC voltage V is applied across it causing power dissipation P<sub>1</sub>. Which one of the following is the correct relationship between P and P<sub>1</sub> ?
explanation:
For metallic wire A,

Resistance, RA = $$\rho .{l \over {\pi {r^2}}}$$

Therefore, power dissipation in wire A, when a DC voltage V is applied,

$$P = {{{V^2}} \over {{R_A}}} = {{{V^2}} \over {\rho .{l \over {\pi {r^2}}}}} = {{{V^2}.\,\pi {r^2}} \over {\rho l}}$$

$$P = {{\pi {r^2}{V^2}} \over {\rho l}}$$ ...... (i)

For metallic wire B,

lB = 2l, rB = 2r, then resistance of wire B,

$${R_B} = \rho .{{{l_B}} \over {\pi {r_B}^2}} = \rho .{{2l} \over {\pi {{(2r)}^2}}} = \rho .{{2l} \over {4\pi {r^2}}}$$

$$\therefore$$ Power dissipation in wire B,

$${P_1} = {{{V^2}} \over {{R_B}}} = {{{V^2}} \over {{{\rho \,.\,2l} \over {4\pi {r^2}}}}} = {{2\pi {r^2}{V^2}} \over {\rho l}} = 2P$$ [from Eq. (i)]

$$\therefore$$ $$P = {{{P_1}} \over 2}$$

content:
Consider the following statements about a solenoid.

1. The magnetic field strength in a solenoid depends upon the number of turns per unit length in the solenoid.

2. The magnetic field strength in a solenoid depends upon the current flowing in the wire of the solenoid.

3. The magnetic field strength in a solenoid depends upon the diameter of the solenoid.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
explanation:
Magnetic field strength in a solenoid is given by

B = $$\mu$$0ni

where, $$\mu$$0 = permeability of free space, n = number of turns per unit length and i = current flowing through solenoid.

So, it is clear that magnetic field strength B in a solenoid depends on n and i only, it does not depends on diameter of solenoid. Therefore, statements 1 and 2 are correct.

content: Light year is a unit of measurement of

options.content: amount of light received on earth in a year

explanation:
Light year is a unit of measurement of very large distances.

It is a distance travelled by light in vacuum in one year.

1 light year = 9.46 $$\times$$ 1015 m

content: The focal length of the objective lens of a telescope is 50 cm. If the magnification of the telescope is 25, then the focal length of the eyepiece is
explanation:
Given, focal length of objective lens, fo = 50 cm and magnification of telescope m = 25

If fe be the focal length of eyepiece, then

$$m = - {{{f_o}} \over {{f_e}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 25 = - {{50} \over {{f_e}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {f_e} = - {{50} \over {25}} = - 2$$ cm

$$\left| {{f_e}} \right| = 2$$ cm

content: Which one of the following force is non-central and non-conservative?
explanation:
A force on body which does not always act towards a fixed point, is called non-central force.

The forces that do not store energy are called non-conservative forces.

Work done by non-conservative forces depends on the path of the body moving from one point to another point.

Therefore, frictional force is a non-central and non-conservative force.

content:
Consider the following statements relating to Richter scale.

1. It was devised in 1935 by Charles F. Richter.

2. It describes the quantity of energy released by a single earthquake.

3. Richter scale has no upper limit.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
explanation:
Richter scale is a scale of number used to tell the size of earthquakes.

Charles Francis Richter developed the Richter scale in 1935.

This scale describes the quantity of energy released by a single earthquake.

Numbers for the Richter scale are in range from 0 to 9.

So, here statements (1) and (2) are correct.

content: Suppose there are two planets, 1 and 2, having the same density, but their radii are R<sub>1</sub> and R<sub>2</sub> respectively, where R<sub>1</sub> > R<sub>2</sub>. The accelerations due to gravity on the surface of these planets are related as
explanation:
Acceleration due to gravity on the surface of first planet,

$${g_1} = {{G{M_1}} \over {{R_1}^2}}$$ ...... (i)

where, M1 is the mass of first planet.

But, $${M_1} = {4 \over 3}\pi R_1^3.\,{\rho _1}$$ ($$\because$$ mass = volume $$\times$$ density)

where, $$\rho$$1 = density of the first planet.

$${g_1} = {{G\,.\,{4 \over 3}\pi R_1^3.\,{\rho _1}} \over {{R_1}^2}}$$ [from Eq. (i)]

$${g_1} = {4 \over 3}G\pi {\rho _1}{R_1}$$ ..... (ii)

Similarly, acceleration due to gravity on the surface of second planet,

$${g_2} = {4 \over 3}G\pi {\rho _2}{R_2}$$ ...... (iii)

$$\therefore$$ From Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get

$${{{g_1}} \over {{g_2}}} = {{{\rho _1}{R_1}} \over {{\rho _2}{R_2}}} = {{{R_1}} \over {{R_2}}}$$ ($$\because$$ $$\rho$$1 = $$\rho$$2)

Since, R1 > R2, hence g1 > g2.

content: The sun is seen little before it rises and for a short while after it sets. This is because of

options.content: apparent shift in the direction of sun

explanation:
The sun is seen little before it rises and for short while after it sets due to atmospheric refraction.

When light enters from vacuum to earth's atmosphere, it basically enters from rarer to denser medium and bends towards horizon.

During sunrise, when the sun is just below the horizon, our atmosphere causes the light rays to bend and we see the sun early. Similarly, at sunset, the apparent position of the sun is visible to us and not the actual position due to the same bending of light rays effect.

content:
The figure shown below gives the time (t) versus position (x) graph of three objects A, B and C. Which one of the following is the correct relation between their speeds vA, vB and vC, respectively at any instant (t > 0) ?

Image
explanation:
From figure,

Image

Velocity of object A,

$${v_A} = {{O{A_1}} \over {A{A_1}}} = \cot {\theta _A}$$

Velocity of object B,

$${v_B} = {{O{B_1}} \over {B{B_1}}} = \cot {\theta _B}$$

Velocity of object C,

$${v_C} = {{O{C_1}} \over {C{C_1}}} = \cot {\theta _C}$$

Since, $${\theta _C} > {\theta _B} > {\theta _A}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {v_A} > {v_B} > {v_C}$$

content: 1 dyne (a unit of force in CGS system) equals to

explanation:
The slope of velocity-time graph gives acceleration produced in the moving body.

content: Which one of the following statement regarding a thermo flask is not correct?

options.content: The walls of flask are separated by vacuum and made of glass which is a poor conductor of heat.
The glass walls themselves have shiny surfaces.
The surface of inner wall radiates good amount of heat and the surface of outer wall absorbs some of the heat that is radiated from the inner wall.
The cork supports are poor conductors of heat.

explanation: The thermo flask has the silver coating on the inner surface which prevents heat transfer by radiation. The vacuum between its double wall prevents heat moving by convection. The thinness of the glass walls stops heat entering or leaving the flask by conduction. Hence, option (c) is not a correct statement regarding a thermo flask.

content: Black hole is a

options.content: huge black star which has zero acceleration due to gravity on its surface.
star which has moderate acceleration due to gravity on its surface.
star which has collapsed into itself and has large acceleration due to gravity on its surface.
star which has collapsed into itself and has zero acceleration due to gravity on its surface.

explanation: A black hole is  star which has collapsed into itself and has large acceleration due to gravity on its surface. A black hole forms when the center of a very massive star collapses in upon itself. The region in space of a black-hole, the pulling force of gravity is, so strong that light is not able to escape.

content: The formula for conversion between Fahrenheit and Celsius is $$^\circ$$F = X + (1.8 $$\times$$ $$^\circ$$C).
explanation:
The formula for conversion between Fahrenheit and Celsius is

$${{^\circ C} \over 5} = {{^\circ F - 32} \over 9}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {9 \over 5}^\circ C = ^\circ F - 32$$

$$ \Rightarrow 1.8^\circ C = ^\circ F - 32$$

$$ \Rightarrow ^\circ F = 32 + 1.8^\circ C$$

Comparing with given relation of

$$^\circ F = X + (1.8 + ^\circ C)$$

We get, X = 32

content: When a beam of white light passes through a glass prism, the colour of light beam that deviates the least is
explanation: The wavelength of red colour of light is maximum, therefore when a bam of white light passes through a glass prism, then the light of red colour deviates the least.

content: LIGO stands for

content: The focal length of a concave lens is 0.5 m. The power of the lens is
explanation:
For a lens, power is given by

$$ P = \frac{1}{f} $$

where $f$ is in metre.

Since it is a concave lens, its focal length is negative:

$$ f = -0.5 \, \text{m} $$

Now,

$$ P = \frac{1}{-0.5} = -2 \, \text{D} $$

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{-2.0 \, \text{D}} $$

Option 4

content: The rule to determine the direction of a force experienced by a straight current carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field which is perpendicular to it is
explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Fleming’s left-hand rule

Explanation:

When a straight current-carrying conductor is placed in a magnetic field perpendicular to it, it experiences a force.

To find the direction of this force, we use Fleming’s left-hand rule.

Fleming’s left-hand rule:

Stretch the thumb, forefinger, and middle finger of your left hand so that they are mutually perpendicular.

Then:

  • Forefinger shows the direction of magnetic field

  • Middle finger shows the direction of current

  • Thumb shows the direction of force or motion

Why other options are wrong:

  • Right-hand thumb rule: Used to find the direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.

  • Fleming’s right-hand rule: Used for direction of induced current in electromagnetic induction.

  • Hund’s rule: Related to filling of electrons in atomic orbitals.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Fleming’s left-hand rule}}$$

content: One parsec in astronomical units (A.U.) is about
explanation:
We know:

  • $1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \, \text{light years}$

  • $1 \, \text{light year} \approx 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.}$

So,

$$ 1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \times 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ \approx 20.5 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ = 2.05 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

So, one parsec is about:

$$ 2 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

Correct option: Option 3

content: Which one of the following statements for second law of motion is NOT correct ?

options.content: The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s acceleration
The net force on a body is in the same direction as the acceleration
The time rate of change of momentum is equal to force
The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s momentum

explanation:
The statement which is NOT correct is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4: The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s momentum}}$$

Step-by-step:

According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion:

$$F = \frac{dp}{dt}$$

This means:

  • Force is equal to the time rate of change of momentum

  • If mass is constant, then:

$$F = ma$$

So:

  1. Option 1 is correct because force is proportional to acceleration.

  2. Option 2 is correct because acceleration is in the same direction as net force.

  3. Option 3 is correct because it is the actual statement of the second law.

  4. Option 4 is incorrect because force is not proportional to momentum, but to the rate of change of momentum.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4}}$$

content: The temperature of a body increases  from 310 K to 340 K. The temperature increase in degree Celsius is
explanation:
Temperature difference in kelvin and degree Celsius is the same.

Given:

$$ T_1 = 310\ \text{K}, \quad T_2 = 340\ \text{K} $$

Increase in temperature:

$$ \Delta T = T_2 - T_1 = 340 - 310 = 30\ \text{K} $$

So, the increase in degree Celsius is also:

$$ 30^\circ \text{C} $$

Correct option: 2. 30°C

content: Which one of the following statements for the work done by gravity on a body is NOT correct ?

options.content: Work done is independent of the path followed by the body
Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body
Work done depends on the path followed by the body
Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity

explanation:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Work done depends on the path followed by the body

Step-by-step explanation

Gravity is a conservative force. For a conservative force:

  • Work done does not depend on the path

  • It depends only on the initial and final positions

For gravity, work done is given by:

$$ W = mg(h_1 - h_2) $$

where:

  • $m$ = mass of the body

  • $g$ = acceleration due to gravity

  • $h_1$ = initial height

  • $h_2$ = final height

So:

  • If only the vertical height changes, gravity does work.

  • If the displacement is purely horizontal, then there is no change in height, so work done by gravity is zero.

Checking each option

  1. Work done is independent of the path followed by the body

    This is correct.

  2. Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body

    This is correct.

  3. Work done depends on the path followed by the body

    This is not correct.

  4. Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity

    This is correct.

Final answer

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 3}} $$

content: Which one among the following statements is correct ?

options.content: Pressure has magnitude as well as direction:
As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases
Action and reaction forces act on the same object
Pascal and N/m<sup>2</sup> represent the same unit

explanation:
The correct statement is:

$$\text{Option 4: Pascal and } N/m^2 \text{ represent the same unit}$$

Now let us check each option one by one:

  1. Pressure has magnitude as well as direction

    This is wrong because pressure is a scalar quantity.

    It has only magnitude, not direction.

  2. As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases

    This is wrong.

    In a liquid, pressure increases with depth.

  3. Action and reaction forces act on the same object

    This is wrong.

    According to Newton’s third law, action and reaction act on different objects.

  4. Pascal and $N/m^2$ represent the same unit

    This is correct because

$$1 \, \text{pascal} = 1 \, N/m^2$$

So, the correct answer is Option 4.

content: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
explanation:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

content: In a Telescope, compared to the eyepiece lens the objective lens has

options.content: larger focal length and larger aperture
larger focal length and smaller aperture
smaller focal length and larger aperture
smaller focal length and smaller aperture

explanation:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 1: larger focal length and larger aperture}$$

Step-by-step:

  1. In a telescope, the objective lens is the lens facing the distant object.

  2. Its job is to collect as much light as possible from faraway objects.

  3. So, it must have a large aperture.

Also:

  1. The objective lens forms a real, inverted, and diminished image of the distant object.

  2. For better magnification in a telescope, the objective lens should have a large focal length.

  3. The eyepiece acts like a magnifying glass, so it has a small focal length.

Therefore, compared to the eyepiece, the objective lens has:

  • larger focal length

  • larger aperture

Hence, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

content: The energy, E, of a photon can be expressed as E = hf where f is the frequency and h is Planck’s constant. The dimensions of h are the same as that of
explanation:
Given,

$$ E = hf $$

We need the dimensions of $h$.

Step 1: Write dimensions of energy

Energy has the dimensions:

$$ [E] = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Step 2: Write dimensions of frequency

Frequency is the number of oscillations per second, so:

$$ [f] = T^{-1} $$

Step 3: Find dimensions of $h$

From

$$ h = \frac{E}{f} $$

So,

$$ [h] = \frac{ML^2T^{-2}}{T^{-1}} = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Step 4: Compare with given options

  • Linear momentum:

$$ p = mv $$

Dimensions:

$$ [p] = MLT^{-1} $$

Not same.

  • Angular momentum:

$$ L = mvr $$

Dimensions:

$$ [L] = MLT^{-1} \cdot L = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Same.

  • Displacement:

$$ [L] $$

Not same.

  • Torque:

$$ \tau = \text{force} \times \text{distance} $$

Dimensions:

$$ [\tau] = (MLT^{-2})(L) = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Not same.

Correct option:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 2: angular momentum}} $$

content: The work done by the force acting on an object is zero if the displacement of the object

options.content: is in the opposite direction of the direction of force
is in the same direction of the direction of force
is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force

explanation:
Work done is given by

$$ W = F s \cos \theta $$

where:

  • $F$ = force

  • $s$ = displacement

  • $\theta$ = angle between force and displacement

For work done to be zero,

$$ \cos \theta = 0 $$

This happens when

$$ \theta = 90^\circ $$

That means the displacement is perpendicular to the direction of force.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force

content: The magnetic field inside a current carrying very long solenoid is
explanation:

content: Which one of the following instruments can be used to measure —250°C temperature ?

options.content: By using a mercury based thermometer
By using an alcohol based thermometer

explanation:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: By using thermocouple based thermometer}$$

Step by step:

  1. $-250^\circ \text{C}$ is an extremely low temperature.

  2. A mercury thermometer cannot be used because mercury freezes at about

$$-39^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. An alcohol thermometer can measure lower temperatures than mercury, but not as low as $-250^\circ \text{C}$.

  2. A clinical thermometer is used only for measuring human body temperature, usually in the range

$$35^\circ \text{C} \text{ to } 42^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. A thermocouple thermometer is used for very high as well as very low temperatures.

Therefore, to measure $-250^\circ \text{C}$, we use

$$\boxed{\text{thermocouple based thermometer}}$$

content: The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without changing the temperature, is called
explanation:
The correct answer is Option 2: specific latent heat.

Explanation:

The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without any change in temperature is called specific latent heat of vaporisation.

In NCERT terms:

  • Latent heat: Heat absorbed or released during change of state.

  • Specific latent heat: Heat required for unit mass of a substance to change its state without change in temperature.

So here, since the change is from liquid to vapour, it is specific latent heat.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Correct option} = \text{Option 2} $$

content: Which one of the following statements about surface tension is correct ?

options.content: It will remain same with increase or decrease of temperature
It will always decrease with increase or decrease of temperature

explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: It decreases when temperature increases.

Explanation:

Surface tension is the property of a liquid surface due to which it behaves like a stretched membrane.

When temperature increases, the molecules of the liquid gain more kinetic energy and move more freely. Because of this, the intermolecular force of attraction becomes less effective at the surface, so the surface tension decreases.

Therefore:

$$ \text{Surface tension decreases with increase in temperature} $$

So, the correct option is Option 2.

content: A body sinks in water when

options.content: density of water is greater than density of body
density of water is less than density of body
density of water is equal to density of body

explanation:
A body sinks in water when its density is greater than the density of water.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 2: density of water is less than density of body

Reason:

According to the NCERT concept of flotation:

  • If density of body $>$ density of liquid, the body sinks

  • If density of body $<$ density of liquid, the body floats

  • If density of body $=$ density of liquid, the body may remain suspended

Here, for sinking in water:

$$ \text{Density of body} > \text{Density of water} $$

which means

$$ \text{Density of water} < \text{Density of body} $$

Hence, Option 2 is correct.

content: Why a fielder in the ground gradually pulls his hands backward while catching a fast moving cricket ball ?

options.content: To reduce the acceleration of the ball
To increase the acceleration of the ball
To increase the velocity of the ball

explanation:
The correct answer is Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball.

Explanation:

When a fielder catches a fast-moving cricket ball, the ball has a large momentum. To stop it, the fielder has to bring its momentum to zero.

We know:

$$ \text{Force} = \frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t} $$

where:

  • $\Delta p$ = change in momentum

  • $\Delta t$ = time taken

The change in momentum is fixed because the ball has to be stopped. So, if the fielder pulls his hands backward, the time of stopping $\Delta t$ increases.

When time increases, the force decreases.

Also, since acceleration is related to force by

$$ F = ma $$

less force means less acceleration (actually less retardation).

So, by moving his hands backward, the fielder reduces the acceleration of the ball, and therefore the force on his hands becomes less. This prevents injury.

Final Answer:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball}} $$

content: Bottom of a tank containing water appears to be raised. It is due to
explanation:

When light rays come from the bottom of the tank and pass from water to air, they bend at the surface because of refraction.

Since the rays bend away from the normal on coming from a denser medium (water) to a rarer medium (air), the bottom appears to be at a smaller depth than its real depth.

So, the bottom of the tank appears raised.

Correct answer:

$$\text{Refraction of light}$$

content: For a material under static conditions, which one among the following statements is correct ?

explanation:
